content.full_text:
February 7, 1991

California Fair Political Practice Commission
Legal Department
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Legal Opinion

To Whom It May Concern:

As you know, the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code Section 87100 et seq., requires the members of a city council, the city administrator and certain designated city officials to disclose certain economic interests by filing disclosure statements each year. Persons required to file such statements must disclose income and investments in certain real property.

Government Code Section 87302 specifically requires that the city adopt a conflict of interest code. The city has done so and has designated certain positions within the city and the corresponding disclosure categories for each such position. In developing disclosure categories, the chief of police is expressly included. By ordinance and statutes, the chief of police is a department head charged with the responsibility of managing all those personnel, budget, equipment and materials of the police department. The chief of police is, by the very nature of his position, involved with the process of purchasing equipment, maintenance agreements, budgets, salaries, etc., for the police department.

The police chief has indicated that he believes his position should be excluded from any such disclosure category. He argues that Penal Code Section 832.8(f) protects such information and that such a disclosure constitutes an unwarranted invasion of privacy. He also argues that Government Code Section 3308, the Peace Officer Bill of Rights, expressly protects the right of peace officers from disclosing information dealing with property, income, assets and other sources of income.

California Fair Political Practice Commission  
Re: Request for Legal Opinion  
Page 2 of 2  
February 7, 1991

The police chief has requested that his name be omitted from any disclosure category.

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114, I hereby request on behalf of the City of Grass Valley a written opinion as to whether the chief of police may be designated by the council as one of those officers who is charged with disclosing certain assets by virtue of his position.

Your prompt attention to this matter is much appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
CLAUDE L. BIDDLE
City Attorney

CLB/sh
